title: Substance and function in architecture oral history transcript
creator: Soriano, Raphael, 1907- interviewee
Laskey, Marlene L., interviewer
University of California, Los Angeles. Oral History Program
description: Transcript of a nine-hour interview completed under the auspices of the UCLA Oral History Program

Soriano recalls his early years on the island of Rhodes and his migration to America, his education and the influence of Frank Lloyd Wright, Richard Neutra, and Rudolph Schindler on his work, and his innovations in architecture using steel, aluminum, and prefabricated materials
The Bancroft Library, University of California, Berkeley
Forms part of: Oral History collection, Dept. of Special Collections, University Library, University of California, Los Angeles
